actionUnits.condition: GvrReticlePointer (fileID 95) under Main Camera has MonoBehaviour id 926 mapped to script GUID e7dcc90c2e60c4011896c7f21fc1f557 (GvrReticlePointer). Scene settings match script fields: reticleSegments=20, reticleGrowthSpeed=8. Calling SetAsMainPointer() should assign GvrPointerManager.Pointer to this reticle implementation so downstream raycasters can operate.

GvrControllerPointer (fileID 25) under Player has MonoBehaviour id 896 mapped to script GUID f3bf9c1bacbb04f72bb43e892c3a98a9, which corresponds to GvrControllerVisualManager. The scene meta shows this is the exact attached script with no serialized fields. This script only contains Unity lifecycle methods (Start, Update) and private helpers (IsControllerConnected, SetChildrenActive) guarded by preprocessor defines UNITY_HAS_GOOGLEVR && (UNITY_ANDROID || UNITY_EDITOR). There are no public no-arg methods to invoke, and the object has no Collider/Rigidbody/EventTrigger. To achieve code coverage for the connection toggle logic, we need either: (a) a public no-arg test hook on this component, (b) another component on this object (or its children 527/365) exposing a public no-arg method, or (c) a way to simulate GvrController.State changes.

MonoBehaviour id 915 on ddcontroller exactly matches the attached script GvrControllerVisual (m_Script guid aa952cbcc0eb13d4ca558b6da550ff55). The scene meta shows the serialized fields touchPoint (fileID 109), material_idle (guid 3b4f6031d569a624ba87b631c93a788a), material_app (guid 3fe59d3fe0697b84196c28c184197906), material_system (guid 6c9fcd230d4724c40af9e27326dc25f1), material_touchpad (guid a89bc9f2db5f60743a95275d0336b6c2), touchTransparent (guid 089662c06964d614db131b6b344431e4), touchOpaque (guid 7bc1b31ae424a1448b1be6be3707ad31), which are the exact settings required by the script. ddcontroller has no Rigidbody/Collider/EventTrigger listed; therefore, to cover GvrControllerVisual logic we must externally trigger GvrArmModel.Instance.OnArmModelUpdate and simulate GvrController state changes. Required coverage scenarios of OnArmModelUpdate: (1) GvrController.ClickButton = true (touchPoint inactive, material_touchpad set). (2) ClickButton = false and GvrController.AppButton = true (material_app set). (3) ClickButton = false, AppButton = false, GvrController.Recentering = true (material_system set). (4) Idle branch with none pressed (material_idle set). For touch feedback: transition IsTouching from false->true with TouchPos varying (e.g., (0,0), (1,1)) to exercise scaling/positioning and wasTouching toggle; then IsTouching true->false to cover scale-down branch. For transparency: set GvrControllerVisual.AlphaValue < 1.0 to hit transparent materials and PropertyBlock path, and AlphaValue = 1.0 to hit opaque path. Note: GvrControllerVisual exposes no public no-arg methods; Start/Awake/OnDestroy are automatic and OnArmModelUpdate is private. Please provide a public no-arg hook on a component (e.g., GvrArmModel or a test harness) to raise OnArmModelUpdate and set GvrController state/AlphaValue, or provide the other attached components (fileIDs 683, 572) if they expose suitable public no-arg methods we can call.

MonoBehaviour id 921 on Laser matches script GvrLaserPointer (m_Script guid 51b65678ccdd949e9a58874d2880c0ef). Serialized fields in scene (laserColor RGBA=1,1,1,0.2509804; maxLaserDistance=0.75; maxReticleDistance=2.5; reticle fileID 176; reticleSortingOrder=32767) exactly match the script’s properties. Laser also has LineRenderer (required by [RequireComponent]) via another component (fileID 810). Calling SetAsMainPointer should assign GvrPointerManager.Pointer = laserPointerImpl. Lifecycle-driven coverage (Awake/Start/LateUpdate) will occur automatically; there are no UnityEvent m_Calls on this object.
